case_studies.title: AI-Assisted Triage Improved Emergency Throughput at StadtKlinik Berlin
case_studies.summary: StadtKlinik Berlin used an AI-assisted triage dashboard to prioritize emergency room patients and cut waiting times while increasing billable services.
case_studies.content: StadtKlinik Berlin partnered with our team to design and implement an AI-assisted triage workflow. We conducted in-context research with nurses and doctors, redesigned the intake screen, and deployed a prioritization model that surfaces high-risk cases. Within six months, the hospital saw shorter wait times, higher staff satisfaction, and a measurable uplift in revenue from better-coded procedures.
key_metrics: 22% reduction in average ER wait time

case_studies.title: Telehealth Conversion Optimization for NorthRiver Health
case_studies.summary: NorthRiver Health streamlined its telehealth booking flow and increased completed appointments while reducing call-center load.
case_studies.content: NorthRiver Health engaged us to understand why patients were dropping off before completing telehealth bookings. Through remote usability testing and funnel analysis, we simplified the appointment flow, clarified insurance messaging, and introduced proactive support nudges. The redesigned experience drove more completed bookings and higher revenue per digital visit.

case_studies.title: Driving Patient Portal Adoption Across a Regional Health Network
case_studies.summary: A patient-centric redesign of the portal experience brought more users online and reduced administrative overhead.
case_studies.content: For Coastal Regional Health Network, we mapped the end-to-end patient journey, with a focus on registration and lab results. By simplifying sign-up, personalizing reminders, and improving mobile accessibility, more patients began using digital channels for routine tasks, reducing pressure on front-desk teams.

case_studies.title: Hospital Supply Chain Optimization for MetroCare
case_studies.summary: MetroCare used predictive insights to balance inventory levels and reduce costly last-minute orders.
case_studies.content: MetroCare Hospitals struggled with frequent stockouts of critical supplies and overstock of low-use items. We integrated purchasing data with clinical utilization patterns and built dashboards for procurement teams. The result was a more predictable supply chain and fewer expensive emergency orders.

case_studies.title: Digital Onboarding Overhaul for a European Neobank
case_studies.summary: FlowBank increased verified account openings and reduced KYC drop-off using a streamlined onboarding flow.
case_studies.content: FlowBank’s growth depended on seamless digital onboarding. We analyzed the funnel, removed unnecessary steps, and added contextual education around KYC. We also implemented progressive profiling to spread out data collection over time.

case_studies.title: Omnichannel Personalization for a Global Retailer
case_studies.summary: UrbanTrail unified online and in-store behavior to deliver more relevant offers at scale.
case_studies.content: By combining loyalty data with browsing and in-store purchase behavior, we helped UrbanTrail deliver tailored campaigns and on-site recommendations. The program drove higher basket sizes and more frequent visits both online and offline.

case_studies.title: Digitized Store Checklists Improved Compliance for FreshMart
case_studies.summary: FreshMart replaced paper-based checklists with a mobile app, reducing errors and closing gaps in store execution.
case_studies.content: Store managers at FreshMart tracked daily tasks on paper, making it hard to measure compliance. We introduced a mobile-first checklist tool with photo proof and real-time reporting. The improved visibility helped central teams target coaching and reduce audit issues.

case_studies.title: Pricing Experimentation Lifted ARPU for B2B SaaS Platform
case_studies.summary: Northline Cloud used rapid experimentation on plan structure and packaging to unlock new revenue without hurting retention.
case_studies.content: We helped Northline Cloud test changes to its pricing page, paywall messaging, and plan bundles. By combining qualitative research with controlled experiments, the team identified price sensitivities and found a mix that increased revenue per account.

case_studies.title: Predictive Maintenance Reduced Downtime in Automotive Manufacturing
case_studies.summary: AutiLine deployed predictive maintenance models and dashboards to spot issues before they caused costly outages.
case_studies.content: By instrumenting critical equipment with sensors and feeding telemetry into a central analytics platform, AutiLine reduced unplanned downtime and extended machine life. Operations managers gained early warnings and clear action lists.

case_studies.title: IoT Quality Monitoring for a Consumer Electronics Manufacturer
case_studies.summary: BrightWave introduced IoT-enabled quality checks to catch defects earlier in the production line.
case_studies.content: We integrated sensor data from assembly lines with quality assurance workflows, enabling BrightWave to identify defect patterns in real time. Dashboards alerted supervisors to anomalies before large batches were affected.

case_studies.title: Risk Analytics Platform for a Global Insurer
case_studies.summary: AlliedSure centralized risk modeling and reporting, improving underwriting speed and consistency.
case_studies.content: We helped AlliedSure combine disparate data sources into a unified risk analytics platform. Underwriters can now explore scenarios, run standardized models, and share results with compliance teams in minutes, not days.

case_studies.title: Cloud Migration Strategy for a Legacy SaaS Suite
case_studies.summary: CoreLedger migrated core services to the cloud with minimal disruption and better observability.
case_studies.content: We designed a phased migration plan, including strangler patterns, observability improvements, and clear rollback paths. Product and engineering teams collaborated on communication plans to keep customers informed.

case_studies.title: Optimizing Donation Funnels for a Global NGO
case_studies.summary: GlobalRelief increased recurring donations and reduced friction for first-time donors.
case_studies.content: Through user research and experimentation on copy, payment options, and suggested amounts, we helped GlobalRelief remove friction in its donation flow. The organization now sees more recurring donors and higher donation values.

case_studies.title: Enrollment Marketing Optimization for a Public University
case_studies.summary: Central State aligned digital campaigns with student intent signals to improve enrollment yields.
case_studies.content: We worked with Central State University to better understand prospective students’ information needs. By aligning content, retargeting, and nurture flows to these needs, the university improved its yield from accepted to enrolled students.

job_postings.title: Product Designer
job_postings.description: We’re looking for a Product Designer to shape end-to-end experiences across our analytics and collaboration products.
job_postings.responsibilities: Collaborate with PMs and engineers to define problems, run discovery research, create flows and interfaces, and support implementation through launch.
job_postings.requirements: 3+ years designing digital products, strong interaction and visual design skills, experience working closely with cross-functional teams.

job_postings.title: UX Designer, Growth Experiences
job_postings.description: Join our Growth squad to design onboarding, activation, and expansion experiences for new customers.
job_postings.responsibilities: Partner with data and marketing to identify opportunities, prototype experiments, and iterate quickly based on quantitative and qualitative feedback.
job_postings.requirements: 4+ years in UX or product design, strong portfolio of growth or funnel optimization work, comfort with A/B testing.

job_postings.title: Senior UX Researcher
job_postings.description: We’re hiring a Senior UX Researcher to lead strategic and evaluative research across our product portfolio.
job_postings.responsibilities: Plan and run mixed-methods studies, synthesize insights into clear narratives, and coach product teams on continuous discovery.
job_postings.requirements: 5+ years in UX research, strong facilitation and communication skills, experience with both B2B and B2C products is a plus.

job_postings.title: Design Systems Lead
job_postings.description: Own and evolve our cross-platform design system, partnering closely with engineering and brand.
job_postings.responsibilities: Define design tokens and components, maintain documentation, run governance rituals, and support adoption across teams.
job_postings.requirements: 6+ years in product or systems design, experience scaling design systems in multi-product environments.

job_postings.title: Visual Designer, Marketing
job_postings.description: The Visual Designer will craft compelling visuals for campaigns, landing pages, and sales collateral.
job_postings.responsibilities: Create campaign concepts, work with copywriters and marketers, and ensure brand consistency across channels.
job_postings.requirements: 3+ years in visual or marketing design, strong command of layout, typography, and illustration.

job_postings.title: Product Designer, Collaboration Tools
job_postings.description: Join our Berlin hub to design collaboration workflows used by distributed teams around the world.
job_postings.responsibilities: Partner with PM, research, and engineering to define collaboration features and refine flows through prototyping and testing.
job_postings.requirements: 3+ years in product design, experience designing complex multi-user workflows.

job_postings.title: UX Writer
job_postings.description: We’re seeking a UX Writer to define product copy standards and craft clear, considerate in-product messaging.
job_postings.responsibilities: Partner with designers and PMs to write product copy, error messages, and in-app education content.
job_postings.requirements: 3+ years in UX writing or content design, strong portfolio demonstrating problem-solving through language.

job_postings.title: Backend Engineer, Data Platform
job_postings.description: Help build and scale our core data processing and analytics infrastructure.
job_postings.responsibilities: Design and implement services, optimize performance, and collaborate with data scientists and product teams.
job_postings.requirements: 5+ years in backend development, experience with distributed systems and event pipelines.

job_postings.title: Frontend Engineer
job_postings.description: Build delightful, accessible interfaces for our web applications.
job_postings.responsibilities: Collaborate with designers to implement responsive UI components and ensure high performance.
job_postings.requirements: 3+ years in frontend engineering, strong experience with modern JS frameworks.

job_postings.title: Product Manager, Analytics
job_postings.description: Own the roadmap for our core analytics capabilities, from instrumentation to reporting.
job_postings.responsibilities: Define outcomes, prioritize work, and partner with engineering, design, and GTM teams to launch impactful features.
job_postings.requirements: 4+ years in product management, experience with analytics or data products.

job_postings.title: Account Executive, Mid-Market
job_postings.description: Drive net-new revenue and expansion across our mid-market customer segment.
job_postings.responsibilities: Own the full sales cycle from qualification to close, and partner with CS on handoff.
job_postings.requirements: 3+ years in B2B SaaS sales, strong track record of meeting or exceeding quota.

job_postings.title: Marketing Automation Specialist
job_postings.description: Own our marketing automation platform and nurture programs across the funnel.
job_postings.responsibilities: Set up campaigns, manage lead scoring, and partner with sales on MQL definitions.
job_postings.requirements: 3+ years working with marketing automation tools, strong understanding of B2B funnels.

job_postings.title: Customer Success Manager
job_postings.description: Help our customers realize value from our platform and expand their usage over time.
job_postings.responsibilities: Own a portfolio of customers, run QBRs, and collaborate with product to advocate for customer needs.
job_postings.requirements: 3+ years in customer success or account management, ideally with analytics or productivity tools.

job_postings.title: Talent Partner
job_postings.description: Partner with hiring managers in EMEA to build diverse, high-performing teams.
job_postings.responsibilities: Own end-to-end recruitment for key roles, advise on hiring plans, and support employer branding initiatives.
job_postings.requirements: 4+ years in talent acquisition, experience hiring for product and engineering roles preferred.

sustainability_reports.title: 2023 Sustainability Impact Report
sustainability_reports.summary: Our 2023 Sustainability Impact Report details our progress on emissions, diversity, and responsible product practices.
sustainability_reports.content: The 2023 Impact Report covers Scope 1, 2, and 3 emissions, our renewable energy procurement, and case studies from customer projects with positive social impact. It also outlines our governance structure for ESG topics and highlights areas where we fell short of our goals.

sustainability_reports.title: 2024 ESG Overview
sustainability_reports.summary: A concise overview of our 2024 performance across environmental, social, and governance topics.
sustainability_reports.content: The 2024 ESG Overview summarizes key metrics, including emissions intensity, board diversity, and community investment. It is designed for investors and other stakeholders who need a high-level view of our progress.

sustainability_reports.title: 2022 Corporate Social Responsibility Report
sustainability_reports.summary: Our 2022 CSR Report focuses on employee wellbeing, community partnerships, and ethical product design.
sustainability_reports.content: In 2022 we expanded our volunteer programs and launched new internal training on inclusive design. The report includes stories from teams across regions and outlines our early work on supply chain transparency.

sustainability_reports.title: 2021 Sustainability Impact Report
sustainability_reports.summary: Our first comprehensive sustainability impact report, establishing baselines for key metrics.
sustainability_reports.content: The 2021 Impact Report sets out our initial emissions inventory, diversity benchmarks, and commitments around product ethics. It provides the baseline for our subsequent targets.

team_members.title: Head of UX
team_members.bio: Lena leads the UX organization, spanning research, product design, and content design. She has spent over a decade designing complex tools for healthcare and analytics teams and is passionate about inclusive, evidence-based design practices.

team_members.title: Chief Executive Officer
team_members.bio: Nora sets the company’s strategic direction and works closely with customers and partners to ensure our products solve meaningful problems.

team_members.title: VP of Product & Design
team_members.bio: Jonas leads the product management and design teams, ensuring we balance ambitious vision with highly usable experiences.

team_members.title: Director of Product Design
team_members.bio: Priya manages our product design managers and helps teams deliver coherent experiences across the platform.

team_members.title: Head of Analytics Education
team_members.bio: Maya leads our education and enablement programs, helping customers get the most out of their analytics data.

team_members.title: Director of Sustainability & Impact
team_members.bio: Helena oversees our sustainability strategy, reporting, and cross-functional initiatives that reduce our environmental footprint.

blog_articles.title: Announcing FlowSense 3.0: A New Era of Collaborative Analytics
blog_articles.summary: We’re launching FlowSense 3.0, our biggest product release yet, with multiplayer editing, smart alerts, and a redesigned workspace.
blog_articles.content: FlowSense 3.0 is our most ambitious product launch to date. Over the last year, our product, design, and engineering teams partnered with more than 40 customers to rethink how analytics work gets done. The result is a new collaborative workspace that feels as fluid as a whiteboard but as rigorous as a BI tool. In this post, we walk through what’s new, why we built it, and how to get started. This product launch also includes updated pricing for enterprise workspaces and a migration guide for existing customers.

blog_articles.title: Async Rituals for Distributed Teams
blog_articles.summary: Replace status meetings with lightweight async rituals that keep remote teams aligned without burning them out.
blog_articles.content: When teams shift to remote work, they often copy-paste their existing meeting cadence into a new context. The result is calendar overload and little time for focused work. In this article, we share a set of async rituals—weekly planning docs, decision logs, and demo threads—that have helped our distributed teams stay aligned while preserving deep work time. We include templates you can copy and adapt within your own tools.

blog_articles.title: Setting Up Focus Hours in Fully Remote Teams
blog_articles.summary: How we use shared focus hours, time-zone windows, and Slack etiquette to protect deep work.
blog_articles.content: Deep work is hard to sustain when your team lives in chat. We share how we introduced focus hours—blocks of time where meetings and non-urgent messages are off-limits—and how we adapted the concept across time zones. The article includes sample Slack guidelines, calendar labels, and a short rollout playbook any team can pilot in under two weeks.

blog_articles.title: How We Run Remote Design Critiques That Actually Help
blog_articles.summary: A practical format for remote crits that keeps feedback specific, kind, and actionable.
blog_articles.content: Design critiques can easily become either echo chambers or teardown sessions—especially when people are joining from different time zones. We share a step-by-step format for running remote crits, including pre-read templates, facilitation tips, and a simple feedback framework that keeps conversations focused on goals and tradeoffs.

blog_articles.title: The Remote Manager’s Playbook for One-on-Ones
blog_articles.summary: Turn remote one-on-ones into a reliable heartbeat for your team, not just another status check.
blog_articles.content: In a remote environment, one-on-ones are often the only dedicated space where people feel safe raising concerns. We outline a lightweight agenda structure, suggested questions, and rituals for keeping remote one-on-ones consistent and human. We also cover how to adapt the playbook for larger spans of control.

blog_articles.title: Planning Mixed-Methods UX Research for Complex Products
blog_articles.summary: Blend qualitative and quantitative methods to answer nuanced product questions without over-researching.
blog_articles.content: Product teams often struggle to decide when to run interviews, surveys, usability tests, or experiments. We share a simple framing for mixed-methods UX research, with concrete study examples from analytics and healthcare products. The article includes a planning worksheet to help you right-size your next project.

blog_articles.title: Recruiting Participants in Hospital Environments
blog_articles.summary: How our research team safely recruits clinicians and patients for studies in regulated healthcare settings.
blog_articles.content: Running UX research in hospitals comes with additional constraints, from privacy to scheduling. We walk through our recruiting playbook, including how we partner with compliance and clinical leadership, set incentives, and keep sessions respectful of clinical priorities.

blog_articles.title: Making Continuous Discovery Work With Busy Product Teams
blog_articles.summary: Practical habits that keep research flowing alongside delivery work—without burning out your team.
blog_articles.content: Continuous discovery sounds great in theory, but in practice many teams either swing between research sprints and delivery sprints, or they give up on discovery altogether. We share the habits we’ve seen work across dozens of teams: lightweight interview cadences, rolling research backlogs, and shared synthesis rituals.

blog_articles.title: Evolving a Design System for Responsive Data Dashboards
blog_articles.summary: Lessons from scaling our design system to support dense, responsive analytics layouts.
blog_articles.content: Data-heavy interfaces stress test any design system. We walk through how we extended our layout, typography, and visualization primitives to support dense dashboards on both large monitors and smaller laptops. The article includes before-and-after examples and a checklist for evaluating your own system.

blog_articles.title: Designing Empty States That Teach, Not Taunt
blog_articles.summary: Empty states are an opportunity to teach users what’s possible. Here’s how we approach them across our product.
blog_articles.content: Most products treat empty states as an afterthought, but they’re often the first impression a new user gets. We share a simple framework—explain, guide, and reassure—for designing empty states that reduce confusion and accelerate activation.

blog_articles.title: Designing Remote-First Workflows in B2B Tools
blog_articles.summary: Why remote-first workflows are different from “remote-friendly” UX, and how we design for them.
blog_articles.content: Remote-first workflows assume that collaboration happens across time zones, devices, and connectivity levels. We explore how this impacts everything from permissions to notification design, with examples from our own collaboration features and customer products.

blog_articles.title: Finding Your North Star Metric in B2B Analytics Products
blog_articles.summary: A step-by-step guide to defining, validating, and operationalizing a North Star metric.
blog_articles.content: Choosing a North Star metric is less about picking a trendy KPI and more about aligning your organization on what value looks like. We share a practical process we use with customers: mapping value exchanges, testing candidate metrics, and designing dashboards and rituals around the metric. The article includes a worksheet and examples from SaaS, marketplaces, and healthcare analytics.

blog_articles.title: Event Modeling Patterns for Product Analytics
blog_articles.summary: Design event schemas that are stable, flexible, and easy for teams to reason about.
blog_articles.content: Good event design makes every downstream analysis easier. We cover naming conventions, entity modeling, and common anti-patterns we see when teams rush instrumentation. The article includes a sample schema for a collaboration product and guidance on how to evolve it safely.

blog_articles.title: Reading Cohort Retention Charts Without Fooling Yourself
blog_articles.summary: Avoid common pitfalls when interpreting retention curves and cohort tables.
blog_articles.content: Retention charts are powerful but easy to misread. We walk through real examples where survivorship bias, seasonality, and product changes distorted the picture—and how to correct for them. You’ll learn a simple checklist for reviewing cohort analyses before making big bets.

blog_articles.title: Measuring Productivity in Remote Teams Without Spyware
blog_articles.summary: An analytics framework for remote teams that respects privacy and focuses on outcomes, not keystrokes.
blog_articles.content: Many remote work analytics tools default to surveillance. We propose an alternative: a lightweight metrics framework grounded in outcomes, reliability, and team health. We share example dashboards and questions to ask before rolling out new telemetry.

blog_articles.title: What We Shared at Our 2025 Company All-Hands
blog_articles.summary: Highlights from our annual all-hands: product roadmap, sustainability goals, and culture initiatives.
blog_articles.content: Our 2025 all-hands brought together teammates from 18 countries. In this recap, we share the themes from the week: our product roadmap bets, updated sustainability commitments, and new programs to support managers of distributed teams.

blog_articles.title: Product Launch: Team Spaces for Remote Workshops
blog_articles.summary: We’re launching Team Spaces, a new way to organize remote workshops, documents, and analytics in one place.
blog_articles.content: Today we’re excited to launch Team Spaces, a new workspace concept designed specifically for remote workshops and recurring rituals. In this post, we cover the core capabilities, early feedback from pilot customers, and how to enable the beta in your account. This product launch focuses on making facilitation easier for distributed design and product teams.

blog_articles.title: Introducing Healthline Dashboards for Hospital Teams
blog_articles.summary: Our new Healthline Dashboards give clinicians and operations leaders a shared, real-time view of patient flow.
blog_articles.content: Healthline Dashboards is a new product line tailored to hospitals and health systems. Built in partnership with clinical and operations teams, the dashboards surface key metrics on patient flow, staffing, and bed utilization. This launch post explains how the new dashboards integrate with existing EHR systems and what early adopters have seen so far.

blog_articles.title: How We’re Approaching Scope 3 Emissions Measurement
blog_articles.summary: A behind-the-scenes look at our early work on understanding and reducing Scope 3 emissions.
blog_articles.content: Scope 3 emissions are often the largest part of a company’s footprint, but also the hardest to measure. In this article, our sustainability team shares how we mapped our value chain, prioritized data sources, and set interim targets. It’s a companion piece to the 2023 Sustainability Impact Report and outlines what’s next for our supply chain work.

blog_articles.title: Designing Inline Signup Forms That People Actually Use
blog_articles.summary: Inline forms can be powerful when they’re relevant, respectful, and easy to complete. Here’s our playbook.
blog_articles.content: You’ve probably seen inline forms asking you to subscribe or sign up in the middle of an article. When done poorly, they interrupt reading and erode trust; when done well, they feel like a helpful next step. We share principles for timing, copy, and data collection that guide the inline “Get analytics tips” form you see in this article, along with A/B test results from recent experiments.
